content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data," but RunSessionManager.swift (App/RunSessionManager.swift) uses CoreLocation with `startUpdatingLocation()` and builds a `route` of `CLLocation` points, and the app description ("RunTracker maps every run with GPS") confirms location is core to the product.

Pierre: Apple's reviewers directly compare the fetched privacy policy text against actual app behavior, and a flat denial of location collection contradicts both the CoreLocation usage in code and the marketing copy promising GPS mapping — this is exactly the kind of contradiction that gets apps rejected under 5.1.1(i). You need to update the privacy policy to accurately disclose location collection (even if only stored locally/iCloud), explain purpose and retention, and make sure this matches the App Privacy "nutrition label" declarations in App Store Connect. Please fix the policy text before submission rather than leaving this mismatch for review.
